rationale_left: Piloting involves significant physical presence, real-time control, safety-critical judgment, and coordination in unpredictable environments, which creates a strong barrier to full AI replacement. However, aviation is already highly automated, and AI can further reshape flight planning, monitoring, training, and some cockpit decision support, so the occupation faces meaningful but not near-term complete automation.

rationale_left: Air traffic control involves substantial information processing on screens, and AI can assist with routing, conflict detection, weather integration, and decision support—potentially letting each controller manage more traffic. But the core job is real-time, safety-critical control of aircraft in the physical world under heavy regulation, where human judgment, accountability, and rapid coordination remain major barriers to full automation.

rationale_left: Agricultural workers’ core tasks are physical, outdoor, and hands-on: planting, harvesting, feeding animals, cleaning enclosures, and operating machinery in real environments. AI may indirectly reduce labor demand through smarter equipment, robotics, and monitoring systems, but it does not directly automate most day-to-day work from a computer.

rationale_left: This work is overwhelmingly physical, site-based, and equipment-focused, often in hazardous outdoor or offshore environments where human presence, manual skill, and crew coordination are essential. AI may reduce some labor indirectly through automated drilling, robotics, and remote monitoring, but it does not strongly automate the core hands-on tasks of operating, moving, repairing, and maintaining oilfield equipment.

rationale_left: Medical scientists do substantial computer-based work—analyzing data, reviewing literature, designing studies, writing grants, and publishing findings—so AI can significantly boost productivity in important parts of the job. But core research still depends heavily on physical lab work, clinical trials, handling biological samples, and high-stakes scientific judgment, which limits full automation.

title_left: Film and video editors and camera operators
rationale_left: AI exposure is very high overall because film and video editing is almost entirely digital, and AI can already assist with organizing footage, rough cuts, color/audio cleanup, effects, and content generation. Exposure is somewhat tempered by camera-operator work, which still requires physical presence, equipment handling, and creative on-set judgment, but robotic cameras and workflow automation will still reduce labor needs and reshape the occupation.

rationale_left: AI can meaningfully assist with lesson planning, creating instructional materials, grading, and some tutoring or content delivery. But career and technical education teachers still rely heavily on in-person instruction, hands-on demonstrations, classroom management, and supervision of tools, equipment, and safety procedures, which limits full automation.

rationale_left: Veterinarians have meaningful AI exposure in diagnostic support, imaging interpretation, recordkeeping, treatment planning, and client communication, all of which can improve productivity. But the core of the job still requires physical examination, surgery, procedures, emergency response, and in-person judgment with animals and owners, which creates a strong barrier to full automation.

rationale_left: This job mixes digital analysis—studying workflows, preparing charts, interpreting drawings, and analyzing costs—with substantial on-site observation of manufacturing processes, equipment, and workers. AI can significantly assist with data analysis, scheduling, simulation, reporting, and process optimization, but the occupation still depends on physical-world context, plant-specific judgment, and collaboration on the factory floor.

rationale_left: The core of this job is physical, place-based, and highly interpersonal: escorting groups, managing logistics in real time, and engaging visitors on-site. AI can help with peripheral tasks like research, itinerary planning, translation, marketing, and recordkeeping, but it is unlikely to replace the in-person presence, adaptability, and customer interaction at the center of the occupation.

title_left: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
rationale_left: This job combines significant in-person teaching, motivation, and cultural sensitivity with substantial knowledge-work tasks such as lesson planning, assessment, progress monitoring, and individualized instruction. AI can increasingly handle tutoring, translation, practice exercises, and administrative prep, which could materially raise teacher productivity, but the core role still depends on human classroom management, adaptation, and relationship-building.

rationale_left: Retail sales work depends heavily on physical presence in stores, face-to-face customer service, stocking, and handling merchandise, which limits direct automation by AI. However, AI and related technologies can reduce labor needs indirectly through better online shopping, automated checkout, inventory systems, and product recommendation tools, so the occupation faces meaningful but not extreme exposure.

rationale_left: Special education teaching includes substantial knowledge work that AI can assist with, such as drafting IEPs, adapting lessons, preparing materials, tracking progress, and handling documentation. But the core job still depends heavily on in-person instruction, behavioral support, individualized judgment, and close human relationships with students, parents, and staff, which limits full automation.

rationale_left: This is highly digital knowledge work: analyzing IT needs, evaluating projects, coordinating technical teams, and communicating plans are all areas where AI can significantly augment productivity. But the role also depends heavily on leadership, organizational judgment, vendor negotiation, and accountability for business-critical decisions, which makes full automation less likely than for purely individual contributor digital jobs.

rationale_left: This job’s core work happens in the physical world: inspecting materials, measuring parts, monitoring production lines, and handling products on-site. AI and machine vision can automate parts of defect detection, reporting, and process monitoring, but human inspectors still need physical presence, judgment, and hands-on verification in manufacturing environments.

rationale_left: Fundraising combines highly AI-exposed digital tasks—researching donors, drafting messages, analyzing campaign results, managing records, and running online outreach—with relationship-driven work that still benefits from human trust and persuasion. AI is likely to make fundraisers significantly more productive and reduce labor needed for routine prospecting and communications, but in-person donor cultivation and event-based fundraising keep exposure below the highest tier.

rationale_left: Flight attendants’ core work is physical and in-person: ensuring passenger safety, responding to emergencies, enforcing regulations, and providing onboard service in a live aircraft environment. AI may automate peripheral tasks such as reporting, scheduling, or some customer communication, but it cannot replace the legal, physical, and interpersonal presence required onboard.

rationale_left: Registered nurses have meaningful exposure because AI can assist with documentation, patient monitoring, care planning, diagnostic support, and analyzing test results, making the information-heavy parts of the job more efficient. title_left: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rationale_left: This work is centered on physical fabrication, repair, finishing, and quality inspection of medical devices, dentures, and lenses, which limits direct AI automation because it requires hands-on dexterity, specialized materials handling, and use of tools and machinery. AI and related automation can still materially reshape the occupation through CAD software, 3D printing, and automated lens or dental part production, increasing productivity and reducing labor needs in some specialties.

title_left: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rationale_left: This work is dominated by in-person physical tasks: taking and delivering orders, preparing simple items, cleaning tables, stocking stations, and interacting with customers on site. AI may reduce some demand at the margins through self-order kiosks, reservation systems, and automated payment/order handling, but it does not directly replace most core duties without broader robotics adoption.
